title: WakeLockWarning — Screen-Lock Risk Banner Before Recording
problem: iOS below 16.4 and any browser where Wake Lock API is denied will allow the screen to lock during recording. When the screen locks, iOS stops MediaRecorder — the recording silently ends. The user doesn't know until they look at their phone and find a partial clip. This warning must appear BEFORE recording starts, not mid-session.
analysis:
WakeLockWarning checks Wake Lock API availability on mount. Two cases require a warning:
1. `'wakeLock' in navigator === false` — API not available (iOS < 16.4, some Android browsers)
2. Wake Lock permission denied (detected after attempting to acquire)

When a warning is needed: render a dismissible amber banner ABOVE the record button, before any recording starts:
`⚠ Your screen may lock during recording. Keep your phone awake manually.`
Amber background (bg-accent-dim), text-primary, text-sm. Dismiss × button (right side, 32px).

The banner is informational — it does not block recording. Tap × to dismiss (does not persist dismissal — shows again on next visit since the risk is always present on unsupported devices).

TabSwitch/AppSwitch handling: WakeLockWarning also listens for `visibilitychange`. If `document.hidden` becomes true mid-recording, emit a `recordingInterrupted` event to the parent recording page. Parent shows the Post-Record screen (see recording page for full interrupt handling).
successCriteria:
- On mount: check 'wakeLock' in navigator
- If unsupported: banner renders above record button before first recording tap
- Banner: amber bg, dismiss × button, warning text
- Banner does not block record button tap — it's above, not overlapping
- On dismiss: banner hides for this session
- During recording: visibilitychange listener active. On page hidden: emit onInterrupt()
- onInterrupt() handled by recording page (shows post-record screen with partial clip)
implementation:
TODO:
1. components/recording/WakeLockWarning/WakeLockWarning.tsx: props { recording: boolean, onInterrupt: () => void }. Checks `'wakeLock' in navigator` on mount. State: visible (bool), dismissed (bool). When !dismissed && !supported: render banner. useEffect on recording: when recording = true, add visibilitychange listener → if hidden, call onInterrupt().

decisions.decision: Warning appears before first tap, not on Wake Lock acquisition failure
decisions.rationale: Wake Lock can only be acquired after recording starts (or after a user gesture). Waiting until acquisition to show a warning means the user may already be mid-take when they learn their screen can lock. Showing the banner on mount (before any tap) gives them time to read and prepare.
decisions.recommendation: Check `'wakeLock' in navigator` synchronously on mount — no async needed for this check. Acquisition attempt happens in the parent recording page.

knownLimitations: Wake Lock availability check (`'wakeLock' in navigator`) does not guarantee successful acquisition — even supported browsers may deny Wake Lock in low-power mode
No re-show after dismiss: if user dismisses and then the screen locks, they may be confused
